channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: The MSP Guide to Using AI-Powered Risk Management to Scale Cybersecurity
item.description: Scaling cybersecurity services as an MSP or MSSP requires technical expertise and a business model that delivers measurable value at scale. Risk-based cybersecurity is the foundation of that model. Wh...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Iran-Linked MuddyWater Hackers Target U.S. Networks With New Dindoor Backdoor
item.description: New research from Broadcom's Symantec and Carbon Black Threat Hunter Team has discovered evidence of an Iranian hacking group embedding itself in several U.S. companies' networks, including banks, air...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ghanain man pleads guilty to role in $100 million fraud ring
item.description: A Ghanaian national pleaded guilty to his role in a massive fraud ring that stole over $100 million from victims across the United States through business email compromise attacks and romance scams. [...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI investigates breach of surveillance and wiretap systems
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) confirmed on Thursday that it's investigating a breach that affected systems used to manage surveillance and wiretap warrants. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Use TernDoor, PeerTime, BruteEntry in South American Telecom Attacks
item.description: A China-linked advanced persistent threat (APT) actor has been targeting critical telecommunications infrastructure in South America since 2024, targeting Windows and Linux systems and edge devices wi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Reveals ClickFix Campaign Using Windows Terminal to Deploy Lumma Stealer
item.description: Microsoft on Thursday disclosed details of a new widespread ClickFix social engineering campaign that has leveraged the Windows Terminal app as a way to activate a sophisticated attack chain and deplo...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hikvision and Rockwell Automation CVSS 9.8 Flaws Added to CISA KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Hikvision and Rockwell Automation products to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Chinese state hackers target telcos with new malware toolkit
item.description: A China-linked advanced persistent threat actor tracked as UAT-9244 has been targeting telecommunication service providers in South America since 2024, compromising Windows, Linux, and network-edge de...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Bing AI promoted fake OpenClaw GitHub repo pushing info-stealing malware
item.description: Fake OpenClaw installers hosted in GitHub repositories and promoted by Microsoft Bing's AI-enhanced search feature instructed users to run commands that deployed information stealers and proxy malware...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Nation-State Actor Embraces AI Malware Assembly Line
item.description: Pakistan's APT36 threat group has begun using vibe-coding to churn out mediocre malware, but at a scale that could overwhelm defenses....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Tycoon 2FA Goes Boom as Europol, Vendors Bust Phishing Platform
item.description: The phishing-as-a-service platform was popular among cyber threat actors because of its ability to bypass multifactor authentication defenses....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Wikipedia hit by self-propagating JavaScript worm that vandalized pages
item.description: The Wikimedia Foundation suffered a security incident today after a self-propagating JavaScript worm began vandalizing pages and modifying user scripts across multiple wikis. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cisco Drops 48 New Firewall Vulnerabilities, 2 Critical
item.description: Edge bugs are so fetch, and Cisco just dropped 50 new ones, including some heavy hitters with 10 out of 10 scores on the CVSS scale....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: WordPress membership plugin bug exploited to create admin accounts
item.description: Hackers are exploiting a critical vulnerability in the User Registration & Membership plugin, which is installed on more than 60,000 WordPress sites. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI arrests suspect linked to $46M crypto theft from US Marshals
item.description: ​A U.S. government contractor's son, accused of stealing more than $46 million in cryptocurrency from the U.S. Marshals Service, was arrested Wednesday on the island of Saint Martin. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Software Development Practices Help Enterprises Tackle Real-Life Risks
item.description: Organizations can borrow secure-by-design processes to manage non-technical challenges like governance or the inevitable human error....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Cisco Confirms Active Exploitation of Two Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Vulnerabilities
item.description: Cisco has disclosed that two more vulnerabilities affecting Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (formerly SD-WAN vManage) have come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerabilities in question are liste...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Preparing for the Quantum Era: Post-Quantum Cryptography Webinar for Security Leaders
item.description: Most organizations assume encrypted data is safe. But many attackers are already preparing for a future where today’s encryption can be broken. Instead of trying to decrypt information now, they are c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google says 90 zero-days were exploited in attacks last year
item.description: Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) tracked 90 zero-day vulnerabilities actively exploited throughout 2025, almost half of them in enterprise software and appliances. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 2026 Browser Data Reveals Major Enterprise Security Blind Spots
item.description: The browser is becoming the operating system for modern work, yet many enterprises still treat it as an extension of network or endpoint security. Keep Aware's 2026 State of Browser Security Report sh...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: LatAm Now Faces 2x More Cyberattacks Than US
item.description: Much of Central and South America struggles with cybersecurity maturity, and hackers are taking advantage....

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: DDR5 Bot Scalping, Samsung TV Tracking, Reddit Privacy Fine & More
item.description: Some weeks in cybersecurity feel routine. This one doesn’t. Several new developments surfaced over the past few days, showing how quickly the threat landscape keeps shifting. Researchers uncovered fre...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Police dismantles online gambling ring exploiting Ukrainian women
item.description: Spanish and Ukrainian law enforcement authorities dismantled a criminal ring that exploited war-displaced Ukrainian women to run an online gambling scheme that laundered nearly €4.75 million in illici...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Dust Specter Targets Iraqi Officials with New SPLITDROP and GHOSTFORM Malware
item.description: A suspected Iran-nexus threat actor has been attributed to a campaign targeting government officials in Iraq by impersonating the country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs to deliver a set of never-before...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Where Multi-Factor Authentication Stops and Credential Abuse Starts
item.description: Organizations typically roll out multi-factor authentication (MFA) and assume stolen passwords are no longer enough to access systems. In Windows environments, that assumption is often wrong. Attacker...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco flags more SD-WAN flaws as actively exploited in attacks
item.description: ​Cisco has flagged two Catalyst SD-WAN Manager security flaws as actively exploited in the wild, urging administrators to upgrade vulnerable devices. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: APT28-Linked Campaign Deploys BadPaw Loader and MeowMeow Backdoor in Ukraine
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Russian cyber campaign that has targeted Ukrainian entities with two previously undocumented malware families named BadPaw and MeowMeow. "The ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Phobos ransomware admin pleads guilty to wire fraud conspiracy
item.description: A Russian national pleaded guilty to a wire fraud conspiracy charge related to his role in administering the Phobos ransomware operation, which breached hundreds of victims worldwide. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Europol-Led Operation Takes Down Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service Linked to 64,000 Attacks
item.description: Tycoon 2FA, one of the prominent phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) toolkits that allowed cybercriminals to stage adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) credential harvesting attacks at scale, was dismantled by a ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: FBI and Europol Seize LeakBase Forum Used to Trade Stolen Credentials
item.description: A joint law enforcement operation has dismantled LeakBase, one of the world's largest online forums for cybercriminals to buy and sell stolen data and cybercrime tools. The LeakBase forum, per the U.S...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
